content: Queue-based animation sequencing is the standard approach for turn-based RPG combat. The pattern: a CombatAnimator class maintains an internal queue of AnimationStep objects. Each step has: type (attack, hit, heal, block, death), target entity ID, source entity ID, timing (delay/offset), duration, and callback data. The queue processes one step at a time — when an animation completes (via CSS animationend event or setTimeout fallback), the next step dequeues and plays. This prevents the overlapping issue seen with nested setTimeout chains. For idle RPGs where multiple entities act simultaneously, a parallel queue system works: steps can be annotated as 'parallel' (play together) vs 'sequential' (wait for previous). The combat diff from the server is translated into steps by a diff interpreter function — not by imperative setTimeout spaghetti.
